original: RMS Titanic, the world's largest ocean liner, hits an iceberg at 11:40pm off Newfoundland and sinks in the early hours of April 15
The first Space Shuttle, Columbia, returns to Earth after 2 days and 6 hours in space
The Human Genome Project is completed with 99% of the human genome sequenced to an accuracy of 99.99%
"Bill Nyethe Science Guy" starring Bill Nye debuts on KCTS-TV in the US
First public showing ofThomas Edison's Kinetoscope (moving pictures)
Archaeologists announce they have found 3.3 million-year-old stone tools at Lomekwi in Kenya, the oldest ever discovered and predating the earliest humans
Icelandic Volcano Eyjafjallajökull begins erupting from the top crater in the centre of the glacier
European Space Agency's mission to Jupiter's moons, the Juice satellite launches on an Ariane-5 rocket from the Kourou spaceport in French Guiana
Anne Sullivan [Johanna Macy], American teacher who educated Helen Keller, born in Feeding Hills, Massachusetts (d. 1936)
Soviet spacecraft Sputnik 2 with space dog Laika aboard burns up during reentry into Earth's atmosphere
Human cells grown in monkey cells for 20 days reported by US-Chinese team at the Salk Institute in "Cell"
Gerry Anderson, English television producer (Thunderbirds; Space:1999), born in Bloomsbury, London (d. 2012)
Francis Collins, American scientist who led the Human Genome Project, born in Staunton, Virginia
Element 103, Lawrencium, is added to the periodic table
World's first particle accelerator built by Ernest Walton and John Cockcroft performs the first artificial nuclear fission with Lithium at Cambridge University
Black Sunday dust storm ravages the US Midwest, leading to the region being named the "Dust Bowl"
Soldiers marching on a bridge in Manchester, England, cause it to collapse.
US Patent Office issues Patent No. 558,393 to Dr John Harvey Kellogg of Battle Creek, Michigan for "flaked cereal, and process of making same"
First detective story is published,Edgar Allan Poe's "The Murders in the Rue Morgue" [April 1841]
Seychelles President Danny Faure makes first-ever live speech from a submersible pleading for better marine protection
Ioannis V. Yannas, Greek-American physical chemist and co-inventor of artificial skin, born in Athens, Greece[1]
Arogyaswami Paulraj, Indian engineer and inventor (MIMO Wireless Technology), born in Pollachi, Tamil Nadu, British India[1]
Gregory "Greg" Winter, British biochemist (Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2018) known for the therapeutic use of antibodies, born in Leicester, England
Henrik Stiesdal, Danish inventor and engineer (1st off shore wind farm), born in Hørsholm, Denmark
1st underwater launching of Polaris missile
Beyoncéis the first black woman to headline the Coachella Music Festival, her performance on this day the most-watched performance ever on YouTube
Kendrick Lamarreleases his fourth studio album "DAMN." (2018 Pulitzer Prize for Music)

EMI America Records releasesDavid Bowie's 15th studio album, "Let's Dance"; co-produced by Bowie and Nile Rodgers, it becomes his biggest commercial success with sales of nearly 11 million units, topping the charts in 9 countries, and introduces guitaristStevie Ray Vaughanto a larger audience
Quiet BeatleGeorge Harrisonis impressed by unsigned British blues band "Rolling Stones"
Katsuhiro Otomo, Japanese manga artist and animator, "Akira", born in Tome, Japan
A flash of light is observed in crater Plato on Moon
Etienne Oehmichen sets helicopter distance record of 358 meters
AbolitionistJohn BrownmeetsHarriet Tubmanat a Constitutional Convention convened in Chatham, Ontario
Noah Websterregisters the copyright for the publication of the first American dictionary titled "An American Dictionary of the English Language"
1st Pony Express rider arrives in San Francisco from St Joseph, Missouri
British Columbia signs historic Gaayhllxid • Gíhlagalgang “Rising Tide” agreement recognizing Haida Gwaii's Aboriginal title over more than 200 islands off Canada’s west coast
Word "telescope" is 1st used by Prince Federico Cesi
Dr. Harry Plotz isolates the bacteria that causes typhus fever at Mount Sinai Hospital in NYC
RCA demonstrated its new electron microscope in Philadelphia
Ampex Corp demonstrates 1st commercial videotape recorder
US PresidentWilliam Howard Taftbegins tradition of throwing ceremonial first pitch on Opening Day of baseball season
US head ofFirestoneTire and Rubber Company Stacy G. Carkhuff patents non-skid tire pattern
Desmond Tutuelected Anglican Archbishop of Capetown, South Africa
66th US Masters Tournament:Tiger Woodsbecomes the third player to claim back-to-back Masters, three strokes ahead of Retief Goosen of South Africa
LA Dodgers' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherSandy Koufaxthrows his 9th complete game without allowing a walk in 4-0 Opening Day win over St. Louis Cardinals
Chicago White Sox pitcher Carlos Rodón no-hits the Cleveland Indians, 8-0 at Guaranteed Rate Field, Chicago
First regular-season MLB game played outside the United States; Montreal Expos beat St. Louis Cardinals, 8-7 at Parc Jarry, Montreal
First NBA game at New York's iconic Madison Square Garden; NY Knicks beat San Diego Rockets, 114-102
Detroit Red Wings win NHL record 62 games
Leah Neuberger wins her 8th women's singles ping pong championship
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Montreal Canadiens beat Toronto Maple Leafs, 4-0 for a 4 game sweep; 5th consecutive title
26-year-old catcher/outfielderElston Howardbecomes first African-American to play for NY Yankees; hits single with 1 RBI in 8-4 loss to Boston Red Sox
American record company Motown, founded byBerry GordyJr, is incorporated as Motown Record Corporation in Detroit. Michigan
A severe hailstorm strikes Sydney, Australia causing A$1.7 billion in insured damages, the most costly natural disaster in Australian history
Ahmed Salah wins 1st World Cup marathon (2:08:09)
India beats Sri Lanka to win the Asia Cricket Cup final in Sharjah
Cecil Chubb, English barrister, gifted Stonehenge to the British nation, born in Shrewton (d. 1934)
Ali Akbar Khan, Indian classical sarod player, composer, and music educator, born in Shibpur, Bengal Presidency, British India (d. 2009)
Valentin Lebedev, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 13, 35, T-5), born in Moscow, Russia
Clarice Elaine Gaylord, American environmentalist and the first director of the Office of Environmental Justice, born in Los Angeles, California
